question_text: Which statement correctly describes the relationship between distance, speed, and time?

options.label: B
options.text: Distance travelled equals speed multiplied by time

options.label: C
options.text: Time is always greater than distance

options.label: D
options.text: Speed and distance are not related to time

correct_answer: Distance travelled equals speed multiplied by time
explanation: The relationship between distance, speed, and time is given by the equation: distance = speed × time. This means if you travel at a faster speed or travel for a longer time, you will cover a greater distance.
